verses.text: Caesar's decree sends all to be taxed, each to his own city. Providence at work. Even an emperor's order serves to bring Christ to Bethlehem.
Caesar's decree sends all to be taxed, each to his own city. Providence at work. Even an emperor's order serves to bring Christ to Bethlehem.
Caesar's decree sends all to be taxed, each to his own city. Providence at work. Even an emperor's order serves to bring Christ to Bethlehem.
Joseph and Mary go to Bethlehem; she brings forth her firstborn and lays Him in a manger, 'no room... in the inn.' Lowly birth. The Lord of glory enters the world in poverty.
Joseph and Mary go to Bethlehem; she brings forth her firstborn and lays Him in a manger, 'no room... in the inn.' Lowly birth. The Lord of glory enters the world in poverty.
Joseph and Mary go to Bethlehem; she brings forth her firstborn and lays Him in a manger, 'no room... in the inn.' Lowly birth. The Lord of glory enters the world in poverty.
Joseph and Mary go to Bethlehem; she brings forth her firstborn and lays Him in a manger, 'no room... in the inn.' Lowly birth. The Lord of glory enters the world in poverty.
Shepherds keep watch; an angel brings 'good tidings of great joy'; a Saviour, Christ the Lord, is born. Gospel to the lowly. The first to hear are humble working men.
Shepherds keep watch; an angel brings 'good tidings of great joy'; a Saviour, Christ the Lord, is born. Gospel to the lowly. The first to hear are humble working men.

Shepherds keep watch; an angel brings 'good tidings of great joy'; a Saviour, Christ the Lord, is born. Gospel to the lowly. The first to hear are humble working men.
A heavenly host praises, 'Glory to God in the highest, and on earth peace, good will toward men.' Heaven's song. Christ's birth brings glory to God and peace to men.
A heavenly host praises, 'Glory to God in the highest, and on earth peace, good will toward men.' Heaven's song. Christ's birth brings glory to God and peace to men.
The shepherds go and find Mary, Joseph, and the babe in the manger. Prompt seeking. They lose no time to seek the new-born Saviour.
The shepherds go and find Mary, Joseph, and the babe in the manger. Prompt seeking. They lose no time to seek the new-born Saviour.
They spread the news; all wonder; Mary keeps these things in her heart; the shepherds glorify God. Treasured wonder. She ponders, while they proclaim, the grace of God.
They spread the news; all wonder; Mary keeps these things in her heart; the shepherds glorify God. Treasured wonder. She ponders, while they proclaim, the grace of God.

At eight days He is circumcised and named Jesus, then presented in the temple. Under the law. The Saviour is born subject to the very law He came to fulfil.
At eight days He is circumcised and named Jesus, then presented in the temple. Under the law. The Saviour is born subject to the very law He came to fulfil.

Simeon, told he should not die till he saw the Christ, takes the child in his arms. Patient hope. The waiting saint at last embraces his Lord.
Simeon, told he should not die till he saw the Christ, takes the child in his arms. Patient hope. The waiting saint at last embraces his Lord.
Simeon, told he should not die till he saw the Christ, takes the child in his arms. Patient hope. The waiting saint at last embraces his Lord.
Simeon, told he should not die till he saw the Christ, takes the child in his arms. Patient hope. The waiting saint at last embraces his Lord.
'Lord, now lettest thou thy servant depart in peace... mine eyes have seen thy salvation,' a light to the Gentiles. The Nunc dimittis. Having seen Christ, he is ready to die in peace.
'Lord, now lettest thou thy servant depart in peace... mine eyes have seen thy salvation,' a light to the Gentiles. The Nunc dimittis. Having seen Christ, he is ready to die in peace.
'Lord, now lettest thou thy servant depart in peace... mine eyes have seen thy salvation,' a light to the Gentiles. The Nunc dimittis. Having seen Christ, he is ready to die in peace.
'Lord, now lettest thou thy servant depart in peace... mine eyes have seen thy salvation,' a light to the Gentiles. The Nunc dimittis. Having seen Christ, he is ready to die in peace.
Simeon foretells this child set 'for the fall and rising again of many,' and a sword to pierce Mary's soul. Mixed prophecy. Christ divides hearts, and His mother will share His grief.
Simeon foretells this child set 'for the fall and rising again of many,' and a sword to pierce Mary's soul. Mixed prophecy. Christ divides hearts, and His mother will share His grief.

Anna the prophetess gives thanks and speaks of Him to all who looked for redemption. Faithful witness. The aged widow heralds the Redeemer to the waiting.
Anna the prophetess gives thanks and speaks of Him to all who looked for redemption. Faithful witness. The aged widow heralds the Redeemer to the waiting.
Anna the prophetess gives thanks and speaks of Him to all who looked for redemption. Faithful witness. The aged widow heralds the Redeemer to the waiting.
They return to Nazareth; the child grows strong, filled with wisdom. Quiet growth. The Son of God passes through a real and humble childhood.
They return to Nazareth; the child grows strong, filled with wisdom. Quiet growth. The Son of God passes through a real and humble childhood.
At twelve He goes to the passover; returning, His parents miss Him and seek Him sorrowing. Lost and sought. They learn He is no ordinary child.
At twelve He goes to the passover; returning, His parents miss Him and seek Him sorrowing. Lost and sought. They learn He is no ordinary child.

At twelve He goes to the passover; returning, His parents miss Him and seek Him sorrowing. Lost and sought. They learn He is no ordinary child.
They find Him in the temple among the doctors; 'wist ye not that I must be about my Father's business?' His Father first. Even at twelve He knows His divine calling.
They find Him in the temple among the doctors; 'wist ye not that I must be about my Father's business?' His Father first. Even at twelve He knows His divine calling.

They find Him in the temple among the doctors; 'wist ye not that I must be about my Father's business?' His Father first. Even at twelve He knows His divine calling.
He is subject to them, and 'increased in wisdom and stature, and in favour with God and man.' Perfect growth. He grows in every grace, an example to the young.
